user_story: As a visitor I want to understand FoxDesk Cloud and reach signup or login quickly.
expected_behavior: foxdesk.net shows the SaaS marketing page only; app-only routes redirect to app.foxdesk.net and the page keeps pricing/CTA/legal links public.
retest_status: Retested by public Cloud Playwright E2E for marketing copy, pricing separation, previews, and mobile overflow.
notes: Public SaaS web only, not self-hosted app UI.

user_story: As a visitor I want one clear plan and a direct way to start a trial.
expected_behavior: Pricing CTA opens email-only signup, does not force Stripe before trial, and uses customer-facing copy only.
retest_status: Retested by public Cloud Playwright E2E and email-only signup contract.
notes: Stripe checkout belongs inside trial workspace billing, not the initial CTA.

user_story: As a visitor I want Terms, Privacy, DPA, Refund and Security documents available before signup.
expected_behavior: Legal pages render public customer-facing legal copy without internal implementation details and remain linked from signup/public web.
entry_points: pages/legal.php; pages/signup.php legal links; pages/cloud.php footer
retest_status: Retested by legal copy contract and source-language/product-copy contract.
notes: Subprocessors must not be marketed as a normal public nav item unless required by legal flow.

user_story: As a visitor I want to start a 14-day trial by entering only my email.
expected_behavior: Signup form contains one email field, Turnstile, CSRF and legal text; POST creates a hashed magic-link token and shows a neutral Check your email state.
fix_status: Implemented email-only request flow with hashed magic-link token, resend invalidation and no password/workspace fields.
retest_status: Retested by signup contract, public form hardening, local smoke and real browser POST E2E.
notes: No workspace/name/password fields before email verification.

user_story: As a verified email owner I want the link to create my workspace and sign me in.
expected_behavior: GET signup token validates hash, expiry and consumed state, provisions tenant/admin/default organization/trial, signs user in, redirects to Work and shows first-run setup steps.
fix_status: Implemented verified provisioning, atomic token claim before workspace creation, hidden random password, session login and first-run Work onboarding.
retest_status: Retested by targeted Playwright signup E2E covering POST, hash-only token storage, resend invalidation and verified workspace creation.
notes: Plaintext token is never stored; existing users do not get duplicate workspaces.

user_story: As a user I want a secure sign-in and sign-out flow for my workspace.
expected_behavior: Login verifies password, sets tenant-aware session, supports remember-me safely and logout clears session state.
retest_status: Retested by home redirect contract and full core Playwright login/logout workflow.
notes: Magic-link signup uses login_user_session after email verification.

user_story: As a user I want to recover access when I forgot my password.
expected_behavior: Forgot/reset pages require Turnstile, send a time-limited reset token, validate token hash and update password securely.
retest_status: Retested by public form hardening contract and core Playwright profile/password setup coverage.
notes: Existing signup email requests route here instead of creating another tenant.

user_story: As any user I want each domain to show the correct product surface.
expected_behavior: foxdesk.net serves public site, app.foxdesk.net serves customer workspaces, platform.foxdesk.net serves platform admin only, with redirects preventing context confusion.
retest_status: Retested by platform/workspace host contract and home redirect contract.
notes: Critical to avoid mixing SaaS admin and workspace admin.

user_story: As an agent I want one home screen showing the work that needs attention now.
expected_behavior: Work displays mine, unassigned, overdue, waiting and done_today queues from shared modules and links to the matching ticket view.
retest_status: Retested by work queue contracts, Work page contract, and full core Playwright workflow.
notes: Intake queue details should not create a second confusing agenda.

user_story: As an agent I want new intake work to appear inside Work without learning a second agenda.
expected_behavior: Inbox remains a compatibility route that redirects legacy triage links into Work queues; it is not exposed in sidebar or command palette.
fix_status: Resolved by keeping Inbox as a legacy redirect into Work and removing it from primary navigation.
retest_status: Retested by inbox and core UX flow contracts.
notes: Product risk reduced: intake concepts stay in Work instead of creating a parallel agenda.

user_story: As a returning user I may want analytics, but daily work should start from Work.
expected_behavior: Dashboard remains a compatibility analytics view, is removed from primary sidebar navigation, and is reachable as Analytics from Work/command palette.
fix_status: Resolved by moving Dashboard out of primary navigation and relabeling the route as Analytics where users choose it.
retest_status: Retested by core UX flow and Work contracts.
notes: Legacy analytics surface remains available without competing with Work as home.

user_story: As an agent I want open, waiting, done, archive and all ticket views that never hide completed work from search.
expected_behavior: Tickets list uses shared views and filters; Done/closed tickets remain visible in Done and All.
error_status: Tickets page did not explicitly own the shared closed-ticket visibility decision, so Done/All visibility could drift away from the helper contract.
fix_status: Added explicit closed visibility calculation in pages/tickets.php, passed it into the registry split model, and updated the row view-model contract for the new boundary.
retest_status: Retested by ticket-list views, ticket-row view-model, ticket-list filters, global search contracts, and full core Playwright workflow.
notes: Regression target from previous closed-ticket visibility bug.

user_story: As an agent I want to create a ticket without accidental client assignment.
expected_behavior: New ticket requires explicit or deterministic assignment; if agent/company is unclear the field stays empty and no random company is selected.
retest_status: Retested by core UX flow contract and Playwright regression that creates tickets without carrying over previous company or assignee selection.
notes: Regression target from random company fallback issue.

user_story: As a user I want ticket detail to show title, status, client, assignee, activity and allowed actions consistently.
expected_behavior: Ticket detail context comes from shared read-model modules; layout prevents broken headings and shows correct actions by state and permission.
error_status: Ticket detail surface contract found generated muted metadata using the generic theme muted utility instead of the ticket-detail-muted surface class.
fix_status: Changed ticket detail metadata/history muted text to ticket-detail-muted and kept the shared read-model/surface contracts green.
retest_status: Retested by ticket detail context, surface, sidebar, composer contracts, and full core Playwright workflow.
notes: Needs ongoing visual smoke for long titles and right sidebar consistency.

user_story: As a user I want to reply publicly or internally and preserve readable formatting.
expected_behavior: Composer supports public/internal replies, attachments, status update on submit, email notification policy and formatted content.
retest_status: Retested by ticket composer surface contract, notification policy contract, email notification contract, email formatting contract, pseudo-cron email contract, and core Playwright workflow.
notes: Email body formatting and notification duplication remain high-risk areas for runtime testing.

user_story: As an agent I want Start work, Pause, Complete and Edit to do exactly what the label says.
expected_behavior: Start work starts timer; pause pauses timer; complete moves ticket to a done status without cancelling it and should stop or resolve active timer behavior predictably.
entry_points: ticket detail action buttons; api timer/status endpoints
error_status: Reported workflow failure: Complete had no useful tooltip and could leave a running timer active after completion.
fix_status: Fixed: active timer changes Complete label to Complete & stop timer, sends stop_timer_on_complete, and handler explicitly stops the timer.
retest_status: Retested: ticket-detail-actions contract and local smoke passed.
notes: Keep this in E2E runtime queue for browser verification on a real active timer.

user_story: As an agent I want to track time on tickets and as admin I want that time to feed reports.
expected_behavior: Timer endpoints start/pause/resume/stop/discard; manual time entries can be added, edited and deleted within permissions.
error_status: Report item billing adjustment controls were trapped in the horizontally scrolled amount column and could be unreachable in browser tests.
fix_status: Moved item-level adjustment controls into a full-width row below each billable entry, updated live total JS to resolve forms by entry id, and added E2E for complete active timer.
retest_status: Retested: active timer complete E2E, report item/bulk adjustment E2E, and report module contracts passed.
notes: Keep full attachment/report export flows in the next runtime pass.

user_story: As a user I want to upload, preview, download and delete allowed ticket attachments.
expected_behavior: Upload enforces permissions and size, attaches files to ticket comments or records, and storage backend follows edition rules.
entry_points: ticket detail attachments; api upload; attachment.php; image.php; delete-attachment API
code_evidence: includes/api/upload-handler.php; includes/upload-functions.php; includes/storage-functions.php; attachment.php; image.php; includes/components/ticket-detail-sidebar.php; assets/js/ticket-detail.js
error_status: Attachment flow only had browser proof for upload/download; delete was indirect through deleting a comment and image preview was not covered by E2E.
fix_status: Added explicit delete-attachment API, attachment delete permission helper, sidebar delete action, JS handler, and E2E image preview/delete coverage.
retest_status: Retested: upload two files, download text attachment, open image preview lightbox, delete attachment, assert DB row removed; JS/sidebar/security contracts passed.
notes: External R2 smoke remains tracked separately in STORAGE-001.

user_story: As an agent I want tags to classify and filter tickets consistently.
expected_behavior: Tag API lists and updates ticket tags with normalized shared helper behavior and permission checks.
entry_points: ticket detail/ticket list tags; api get-tags/update-tags
fix_status: Fixed tag autocomplete to use the shared normalizer and added targeted tag coverage.
retest_status: Retested by tag contract and Playwright tag update/filter E2E.
notes: Covers normalized create/update suggestions, permission/CSRF contract and ticket list filtering.

user_story: As a user I want search to find open and completed tickets, clients and relevant records from anywhere.
expected_behavior: Global search groups results by type/status and does not limit results to the current agenda.
entry_points: top search; api global-search; api search-tickets
retest_status: Retested by global search unit, API, UI contracts and full core Playwright workflow.
notes: Regression target from search only showing current/open agenda.

user_story: As an agent I want one client profile with contacts, active/done tickets, time and billing context.
expected_behavior: Client detail reads shared overview stats, contacts, recent work, time this month, billable summary and rates.
retest_status: Retested by client overview contract and full core Playwright workflow including client-specific billable rate setup.
notes: Client should be the center of context, not just a company table.

user_story: As an admin I want to manage companies and their user memberships safely.
expected_behavior: Organization admin surfaces create/update/archive companies and APIs add/remove members with permission and tenant boundaries.
retest_status: Retested by SaaS security boundary contract and permissions Playwright suite.
notes: Naming should present Companies/Clients consistently in source language and translations.

user_story: As an admin I want to manage staff/client users, roles, permissions, 2FA and status.
expected_behavior: Users admin supports CRUD, role/permission changes, impersonation guardrails and archived-user safety.
retest_status: Retested by team users contract, admin/security Playwright suite, and permissions Playwright suite.
notes: Permissions must match permission map and tenant/org scope.

user_story: As an admin I want to create AI agent identities and scoped tokens in one obvious flow.
expected_behavior: AI agent creation/editing exposes token creation, revocation and scopes near the agent, using the creator's permissions and clear copy.
error_status: Reported UX failure: token permissions are not editable during token creation/editing in an intuitive place.
fix_status: Fixed: Agent Connect now shows token action scopes before token generation, persists selected scopes, and reads active token scopes instead of silently using hardcoded defaults.
retest_status: Retested: team module contract, agent API contracts, admin UI contract, PHP lint, and local smoke passed.
notes: Keep live browser UX pass for the wider AI-agent settings surface under VISUAL-001.

user_story: As a trusted assistant I want to create tickets, add time, comment and build reports only within granted permissions.
expected_behavior: Bearer token auth maps to the token owner, enforces action scopes, rate limits, tenant/org scope and audit trail for agent endpoints.
retest_status: Retested by agent API control, quickstart, MCP wrapper, production-smoke contract, and MCP smoke.
notes: Needed for Codex/Claude style assistant operation.

user_story: As a native app client I need stable endpoints for shell, home, tickets, detail, comments, timers, reports and notifications.
expected_behavior: app-* endpoints return tenant-aware JSON contracts without relying on web-page HTML and stay source-language stable.
retest_status: Retested by app contract API, app home contract, and native API freeze contract.
notes: Foundation for future iOS/Android native apps.

user_story: As a mobile user I want secure login, refresh, 2FA and push device registration.
expected_behavior: Mobile endpoints support login, optional 2FA, refresh/logout, current user, register/unregister device and push subscription state.
retest_status: Retested by mobile API contract and native API freeze contract.
notes: Native app work should build on this API rather than scraping web UI.

user_story: As an admin I want to choose client and period and see all billable items for review.
expected_behavior: Report query/filer modules return all matching time entries/items for the selected client/period without hiding items.
retest_status: Retested by report filter and reporting flow contracts plus core Playwright report workflow.
notes: Regression target from missing detailed monthly client items.

feature: Line item adjustments and live totals
user_story: As an admin I want to edit each report line item rate, discount or amount before invoicing.
expected_behavior: Each line item supports rate/discount overrides and totals recalculate live after every edit.
retest_status: Retested by billing review, report adjustment, report totals/export contracts, report billing-review JS contract, and core Playwright bulk adjustment workflow.
notes: Must support per-item changes, not only bulk adjustments.

user_story: As an admin I want to share or export a reviewed client report safely.
expected_behavior: Share/export happens after review, uses scoped public tokens/themes and protects tenant/client data.
retest_status: Retested by report export contract and self-hosted update/report Playwright suite for public report token and expiry.
notes: Public report link expiry and theme should be included in runtime testing.

user_story: As an admin I want different hourly rates by agent, client and ticket/item override.
expected_behavior: Rate resolution supports default agent rate, client-specific agent rate, ticket-specific override and final item-level override.
entry_points: reports billing review; client/admin rate settings
retest_status: Retested by report rate parity contract and core Playwright workflow that sets a per-agent client billable rate.
notes: Required by user billing workflow.

user_story: As a workspace admin I want to see trial/paid/free/past-due state and the next clear action.
expected_behavior: Billing page shows only workspace billing actions, hides platform-only controls, and uses clear customer-facing state text.
retest_status: Retested by billing lifecycle contract and billing usage counters contract.
notes: No confusing internal restricted/activate copy for active/free tenants.

feature: Stripe checkout, portal, tax and VAT
user_story: As a workspace admin I want to add billing details, VAT ID and manage subscription securely.
expected_behavior: Stripe checkout/portal/webhook support trial conversion, VAT/tax, cancellation, failed payment recovery and usage/state updates.
error_status: Production live validation originally failed on idempotent already_reported usage; fixed validate-stripe-usage.php so already_reported is accepted as a valid live validation result.
fix_status: Added and deployed production-safe Stripe billing smoke that creates a temporary tenant/customer, opens Checkout, verifies automatic tax, VAT ID collection, base/storage line items, success/cancel return URLs and Customer Portal host, then expires Checkout and deletes the temporary customer/tenant.
retest_status: Production live smoke passed: key_mode=live, checkout_host=checkout.stripe.com, portal_host=billing.stripe.com, automatic_tax/tax_id_collection/base price/storage price checks true, checkout expired, Stripe customer deleted, DB cleaned. Still needs intentional full checkout completion with card, webhook processing, VAT ID entry/reverse-charge behavior, cancellation, failed-payment and recovery path before marking BILLING-002 retested_pass.
notes: Do not create duplicate live checkout sessions for an active paid workspace without a controlled test tenant/card scenario.

user_story: As Aenze platform admin I want a separate console for all tenants and lifecycle state.
expected_behavior: Platform console is host-separated, compact, platform-admin-only and never shown to normal workspace admins.
retest_status: Retested by platform operator detail contract, operator audit contract, and platform/workspace host contract.
notes: Must remain visually and functionally separate from customer workspace.

user_story: As Aenze platform admin I want to activate, suspend, set free override and inspect billing/migration state.
expected_behavior: Tenant detail shows lifecycle actions appropriate to current state and never offers nonsensical duplicate actions.
error_status: Platform catalog/detail rendered lifecycle buttons from hardcoded status checks, so active/free states could still show duplicate Free access or trial actions.
fix_status: Platform action rendering now uses billing_tenant_lifecycle_state platform_buttons; manual/free tenants only show valid operator actions and active paid tenants do not show trial/reactivate actions.
retest_status: Retested: free override E2E, trial lifecycle E2E, billing lifecycle contract, operator detail contract, and operator audit contract passed.
notes: External Stripe checkout/portal smoke remains tracked separately in BILLING-002.

user_story: As an admin I want to sync my self-hosted FoxDesk to SaaS and then cut over to one running instance.
expected_behavior: Migration connect/plan/status/push endpoints sync users, clients, tickets, comments, time entries, reports and evidence without duplicates.
retest_status: Retested by SaaS migration bridge contract, cloud migration bridge contract, cutover preflight test, and migration ZIP security E2E.
notes: ZIP import remains fallback; API sync is target flow.

user_story: As an admin I want attachments synced with evidence and self-hosted disabled after cutover.
expected_behavior: Attachment sync uses tenant-prefixed SaaS keys, counts bytes/files, avoids duplicate imports and final cutover disables old ingest/notifications.
error_status: Attachment upload bridge had only static contracts and no runtime proof of attachment evidence or duplicate prevention.
fix_status: Added Playwright runtime coverage for bearer-token migration attachment upload source-to-target ticket remapping connection evidence and idempotent repeat upload.
retest_status: Retested by SaaS control-plane E2E posting multipart attachment through migration-push-attachment and checking attachment row object map checksum byte counters and second-run no duplicate behavior.
notes: Production cutover still requires operator evidence from cutover gate/preflight/postcheck before DNS or self-hosted ingest changes.

user_story: As a user I want useful emails, not three emails for one action.
expected_behavior: Notification policy deduplicates user actions so one action produces at most one meaningful email, with consistent templates and readable formatting.
error_status: Reported product failure: creating and assigning a ticket to yourself can send multiple redundant emails with inconsistent formatting.
fix_status: Verified: policy suppresses staff requester confirmations and self-assignment, collapses created+assigned events, and ticket emails use the shared HTML renderer.
retest_status: Retested: notification policy, email notification contract, email formatting, and email voice contracts passed.
notes: If production still sends duplicate emails, verify deployed self-hosted version and email template overrides against this code path.

user_story: As a customer I want replies sent to support email to become ticket replies in the right workspace.
expected_behavior: Cloudflare inbound route authenticates expected webhook, maps address/tenant/thread, creates or updates ticket and stores formatted content.
error_status: Earlier local env was SMTP-only, but production now has Cloudflare provider/account/token/from/reply-to configured.
fix_status: Verified on production: outbound signup/reset/new-ticket/ticket-reply/billing messages sent to lh@aenze.com through direct Cloudflare; inbound archive smoke created a temporary ticket reply, ingested the attachment, wrote raw email and attachment keys to R2, and cleaned DB.
retest_status: Retested by production Cloudflare outbound smoke and bin/test-cloudflare-inbound-archive.php --json --timeout=120.
notes: Production inbound evidence: ticket reply address tickets+tk-718-...@foxdesk.net, raw/attachment archived under foxdesk-email-archive, DB cleaned.

user_story: As a self-hosted admin I want inbound email fetched automatically even without server cron.
expected_behavior: IMAP ingest runs by CLI cron or pseudo-cron at configured interval; manual test remains diagnostics only.
retest_status: Retested by pseudo-cron email contract and email formatting contract.
notes: Self-hosted-only operating mode; SaaS should use Cloudflare inbound.

user_story: As a user I want a bell summary and a full notification page without duplicate navigation.
expected_behavior: Bell shows unread count and links to all notifications; left nav should avoid duplicating the same destination if design requires simplification.
error_status: Reported UX failure: notifications were duplicated in left navigation even though they are also reachable from the top bell.
fix_status: Fixed: removed Notifications from left sidebar, removed sidebar notification badge target, and added View all link in notification panel.
retest_status: Retested: admin-ui contract, app-shell visual contract, and local smoke passed.
notes: Notification page remains reachable from the top bell panel and direct URL.

user_story: As an admin I want to set app name, language, ticket prefix and basic preferences.
expected_behavior: Settings general section saves validated values and keeps helper copy minimal and customer-facing.
retest_status: Retested by settings action contract, settings render contract, admin UI contract, and admin/security Playwright settings layout check.
notes: Avoid duplicate admin nav levels and excessive explanatory copy.

user_story: As an admin I want to configure outbound/inbound email according to edition.
expected_behavior: Email settings validate SMTP/Cloudflare/IMAP fields by edition and provide test actions without exposing irrelevant controls.
retest_status: Retested by settings email contract and email policy contracts.
notes: SaaS managed email should not show self-hosted-only IMAP setup as normal flow.

user_story: As an admin I want to edit notification templates safely.
expected_behavior: Template settings expose supported template types, preserve required variables and avoid breaking transactional emails.
fix_status: Added template validation for allowed keys/languages, required subject/body, unknown placeholders and required variables.
retest_status: Retested by settings email template contract and existing settings module contracts.
notes: UI now marks required variables before save; invalid template saves show a clear error instead of silently passing.

user_story: As an admin I want to configure statuses, priorities and ticket types without leaving settings context.
expected_behavior: Workflow settings link/render workflow CRUD modules consistently and avoid parallel competing menus.
entry_points: pages/admin/settings.php workflow tab; workflow admin pages
error_status: Observed visual failure: admin settings could show two parallel navigation bars, making hierarchy unclear.
fix_status: Fixed: settings page contract keeps global admin-page-nav off the settings surface and preserves only local settings tabs there.
retest_status: Retested: admin-ui contract, app-shell visual contract, and local smoke passed.
notes: Keep broader admin visual audit under VISUAL-001.

user_story: As an admin I want security controls and as a user I want 2FA/profile state enforced clearly.
expected_behavior: Security settings manage role requirements, sessions/security log where applicable and enforce profile setup when role requires 2FA.
error_status: Admin security E2E still expected the old admin-page-nav on Settings, which contradicted the single-navigation rule and produced a false failure.
fix_status: Updated admin/security E2E to assert Settings has admin-tabs and no parallel admin-page-nav, while other admin sections keep the compact admin-page-nav.
retest_status: Retested by security debt contract, admin UI contract, and admin/security Playwright suite.
notes: Security-sensitive; should remain tested after UI refactors.

user_story: As a self-hosted admin I want update checks, logs and backups; as SaaS user I should not manage hosted deployment.
expected_behavior: Self-hosted exposes update/backup/log operations; SaaS hides ZIP update/export as normal workspace agenda.
entry_points: settings system/logs tabs; api update check
retest_status: Retested by settings update contract and self-hosted update/report Playwright suite.
notes: Edition split must keep SaaS deploy centralized.

user_story: As an admin I want custom statuses mapped into simple user-facing groups.
expected_behavior: Status CRUD/reorder works, maps statuses to New/In progress/Waiting/Done groups and preserves Complete semantics.
retest_status: Retested by workflow CRUD contract and shared workflow contract.
notes: Complete must never become cancelled unless explicitly cancelled.

user_story: As an admin I want custom priorities ordered predictably.
expected_behavior: Priority CRUD and reorder APIs save valid values and list them consistently in ticket creation/detail/list filters.
retest_status: Retested by workflow CRUD contract.
notes: Check visual consistency in admin tabs.

user_story: As an admin I want ticket types to classify requests.
expected_behavior: Ticket type CRUD/reorder saves valid active types and exposes them in new/edit/detail/report contexts.
retest_status: Retested by workflow CRUD contract.
notes: Can be simplified if too much admin surface for small customers.

user_story: As an admin I want recurring tickets/tasks generated reliably.
expected_behavior: Recurring task admin defines schedules and background runner creates due work without duplicates.
error_status: Missing runtime proof for recurring duplicate prevention.
fix_status: Added a real E2E runtime check for the CLI recurring runner and duplicate guard.
retest_status: Retested by Playwright E2E running bin/process-recurring-tasks.php twice and verifying tickets plus run history.
notes: Browser test confirms a due task runs once until next_run_date is due again.

user_story: As SaaS operator I want new attachments in R2 with safe tenant prefixes and verified download/preview/delete.
expected_behavior: R2 storage writes tenant-prefixed keys, returns controlled previews/downloads and supports backup evidence outside the app server.
error_status: Earlier local env used local storage, but production is now configured with STORAGE_DRIVER=r2 and bucket foxdesk-production.
fix_status: Verified on production: bin/test-r2-storage.php wrote, read and deleted tenant-prefixed health/upload objects in Cloudflare R2.
retest_status: Retested by production R2 smoke: driver=r2, bucket=foxdesk-production, tenant_prefixed=true, deleted=true, stored_object_deleted=true.
notes: Keep this smoke in the production deployment gate after storage-related changes.

user_story: As self-hosted user I want existing local attachments to keep working and migrate safely later.
expected_behavior: Local storage remains backward compatible, enforces access checks and can be exported/synced to SaaS storage.
retest_status: Retested by SaaS storage/migration contracts and self-hosted core Playwright upload/download attachment workflow.
notes: Edition overlay; avoid forcing R2 into public PHP version.

user_story: As an operator I want a lightweight health endpoint for deployment checks.
expected_behavior: Health endpoint returns a safe status without leaking secrets and works on public/app/platform hosts.
code_evidence: pages/health.php if present; includes/health-functions.php; index.php routing
retest_status: Retested by health endpoint contract.
notes: No auth required but output must be minimal.

user_story: As an operator I want background tasks to run reliably in both hosted and self-hosted modes.
expected_behavior: Cron endpoint/CLI handles email ingest, recurring tasks, maintenance and usage/reporting; self-hosted pseudo-cron covers no-cron hosting.
error_status: Missing runtime proof that CLI maintenance and pseudo-cron endpoint process due work without duplicate reruns.
fix_status: Added E2E runtime coverage for bin/run-maintenance.php JSON output and the protected cron endpoint interval guard.
retest_status: Retested by Playwright E2E covering disabled IMAP state recurring generation token rejection lock release and no duplicate second run.
notes: External server cron scheduling still needs production monitor evidence after deploy.

user_story: As a user I want optional browser push notifications for important updates.
expected_behavior: Push endpoints expose VAPID key, subscribe/unsubscribe and notification delivery within permission and tenant scope.
error_status: Web push subscription state lacked a focused tenant/runtime regression test, and unsubscribe returned a PDOStatement from a bool-typed helper.
fix_status: Added tenant_id to push_subscriptions schema/runtime helper, tenant-scoped CRUD/API notification reads, a focused web-push contract, and fixed unsubscribe to return a real boolean.
retest_status: Retested by web-push contract and targeted Playwright E2E covering VAPID key, subscribe, tenant-bound DB row, unread notification payload and unsubscribe cleanup.
notes: Browser push is optional; actual browser permission UX still depends on user/device support.

user_story: As a user I want to manage my profile, password/preferences and 2FA state.
expected_behavior: Profile page lets user update profile, password, language/preferences and 2FA according to policy.
error_status: Email-only signup users receive a hidden random password, so requiring current password for every password path could strand them in profile settings.
fix_status: Added and translated a profile password setup link that sends the standard reset email, hashes the token, logs security events, and keeps normal password change and 2FA flows intact.
retest_status: Retested by profile password setup contract and targeted Playwright E2E covering profile details, notification preferences, setup-link token generation and 2FA setup start.
notes: Profile still uses password verification for changing email and disabling/enabling sensitive 2FA recovery actions.

user_story: As an authorized user I want to share ticket information safely when enabled.
expected_behavior: Ticket share links use scoped tokens, respect permissions and do not expose unrelated tenant/customer data.
retest_status: Retested by ticket share state contract and security boundary contract.
notes: Retest expiry/revocation if implemented.

user_story: As a self-hosted admin I want a safe guided install on PHP hosting.
expected_behavior: Installer creates config/schema/admin user and force reinstall requires token guard.
error_status: Installer smoke was implicit in E2E setup but not tracked as a standalone feature proof.
fix_status: Added explicit self-hosted installer E2E verifying config.php schema seed settings admin account installed redirect and locked force reinstall.
retest_status: Retested by self-hosted Playwright installer smoke on clean Docker runtime plus security boundary contract.
notes: Not part of SaaS customer workspace.

user_story: As a self-hosted admin I want safe update checks, backup and rollback.
expected_behavior: Updater checks public package, verifies access/CSRF, creates backup, applies update and supports rollback smoke.
entry_points: upgrade.php; update APIs; settings system tab
error_status: Upgrade smoke exposed a MariaDB runtime fatal from parameterized SHOW COLUMNS in upgrade.php; the same unsafe fallback existed in ticket API column detection.
fix_status: Fixed upgrade.php to use column_exists() for api_tokens column checks, fixed ticket API fallback SQL, and added runtime E2E for applying a ZIP update package with backup evidence and rollback cleanup.
retest_status: Retested by self-hosted Playwright update/report suite settings update contracts security boundary full PHP lint and grep scan for SHOW COLUMNS placeholders.
notes: SaaS production uses managed deploy instead.

user_story: As any user I must only see and mutate data I am allowed to access.
